findings.title: Speed-Optimized, Multi-Format Batch Converter
findings.summary: MarkItDown achieves 35–60 files/sec throughput, 100x faster than Docling, 3x faster than Unstructured. Supports 29+ file formats (PDF, Office, HTML, images, audio, URLs). MIT-licensed, v0.1.5 released Feb 2026.
findings.invalidation_condition: If speed advantage erodes due to core algorithm changes or if format breadth no longer differentiates.
findings.decay_rationale: Benchmarks stable 2025–2026; 0.x versioning may change algorithm.

findings.title: Explicit Design for LLM/RAG Output, Not Publication Fidelity
findings.summary: MarkItDown architecture targets semantic Markdown (headings, paragraphs, lists) for LLM consumption, not pixel-perfect publication. v0.1.5 adds optional vision-model OCR (GPT-4o, Claude, Azure DI).
findings.invalidation_condition: If design goals shift toward publication-grade output or if marketing claims change.
findings.decay_rationale: Design philosophy unlikely to change; foundational architecture.

findings.title: Overall Accuracy ~47.3%; Format-Specific Failures
findings.summary: Quantitative benchmarks across 94 real-world documents show 47.3% overall success rate (ChatForest), 25% PDF, 15% OCR. DEV Community benchmark confirms performance degradation >10MB complexity.
findings.invalidation_condition: If baseline accuracy improves significantly (>70%) or if core algorithm changes.
findings.decay_rationale: Benchmarks 2025–2026; stable unless major algorithm changes.

findings.title: Table & Structure Preservation Failure is Architectural
findings.summary: MarkItDown fundamentally fails to preserve tables and document layout. Systenics real-world test: extracts table columns separately ('one column at a time'), destroying row-column correlation. GitHub issue #41 open since 2024, no assignee.
findings.invalidation_condition: If MarkItDown implements table-aware extraction (architectural change required).
findings.decay_rationale: Architectural limitation unlikely to change without rewrite.

findings.title: Text Line Handling Breaks on Unstructured PDFs
findings.summary: PDFs without structural metadata exhibit line-breaking failures. Example: 'Customer Id : 43416064' split across 3 separate lines during extraction, making post-processing difficult.
findings.invalidation_condition: If PDF text layer detection improves in future versions.
findings.decay_rationale: Depends on PDF structure analysis; stable unless core parser changes.

findings.title: Document Complexity Threshold ~10MB
findings.summary: DEV Community benchmark indicates performance degrades sharply on documents >10MB or complex layouts. Exact threshold varies with document structure; requires empirical validation per corpus.
findings.invalidation_condition: If hardware constraints change or if core algorithm optimizes for larger documents.
findings.decay_rationale: Depends on MarkItDown version and document corpus composition.

findings.title: CVE-2025-11849 (Mammoth Dependency): Directory Traversal, CVSS 9.3
findings.summary: MarkItDown depends on mammoth for DOCX conversion. CVE-2025-11849 (CVSS 9.3) affected mammoth v0.3.25–1.10.x: untrusted DOCX with external image links could read arbitrary files (/etc/passwd) or cause DoS. Fixed in mammoth 1.11.0. MarkItDown v0.1.4+ pins mammoth ≥1.11.0.
findings.invalidation_condition: If MarkItDown downgrades or unpins mammoth version.
findings.decay_rationale: CVE status fixed; risk persists only in older versions.

findings.title: XXE Vulnerability in XML Parsing (Fixed v0.1.2+)
findings.summary: MarkItDown had XXE (XML External Entity) vulnerability in DOCX/XLSX/PPTX processing. Fixed v0.1.2 (May 2024) by switching from minidom to defusedxml. Responsible fix; indicates file parsing as persistent risk area.
findings.invalidation_condition: If defusedxml is downgraded or XML parsing is bypassed.
findings.decay_rationale: Fix is stable; risk is historical unless regression occurs.

findings.title: MCP Exposure to Untrusted URIs (SSRF Risk)
findings.summary: MarkItDown MCP server exposes convert_to_markdown(uri) without built-in URI scheme validation. BlueRock research: 36.7% of 7,000+ MCP servers have SSRF vulnerabilities. MarkItDown MCP can read local files (file://) or trigger SSRF (https:// to metadata services). Risk is in MCP implementation, NOT in Python library itself.
findings.invalidation_condition: If MCP server adds URI validation upstream or restricts URI schemes.
findings.decay_rationale: Depends on MCP deployment configuration; can be remediated quickly.

findings.title: Dependency Supply Chain: 25 Dependencies, CVE Surface Area
findings.summary: Installing markitdown[all] pulls 251MB and 25 dependencies (pdfminer.six, mammoth, lxml, pandas, azure-ai-documentintelligence, etc.). CVE-2025-11849 in mammoth is recent example. Regular scanning required. Footprint bloat for containerized pipelines.
findings.invalidation_condition: If [all] install is deprecated or dependencies are consolidated.
findings.decay_rationale: Dependency count stable unless major refactor.

findings.title: Plugin Security Model (Arbitrary Python Code, Default Disabled)
findings.summary: MarkItDown v0.1.0+ introduced plugin architecture. Plugins are arbitrary Python code; disabled by default. Security implications of enabling untrusted plugins not formally documented by Microsoft.
findings.invalidation_condition: If plugin documentation is published or sandboxing is implemented.
findings.decay_rationale: Plugin architecture unlikely to change fundamentally.

findings.title: Heading-Aware Chunking in RAG Pipelines (40–60% Accuracy Boost)
findings.summary: Frank's World blog claims heading-aware Markdown chunking (split at H2/H3 boundaries, preserve metadata) boosts RAG retrieval accuracy 40–60% vs. naive splitting. No methodology disclosed; no independent corroboration.
findings.invalidation_condition: If independent study validates or contradicts claim.

findings.title: Error-Handling Chains & Fallback Strategies
findings.summary: Best-practice RAG integration uses fallback chains: MarkItDown → Azure Document Intelligence / Docling → text extraction. Pattern amortizes speed advantage while catching edge cases. Mentioned by multiple integration tutorials; no formal benchmarks on fallback overhead.
findings.invalidation_condition: If fallback overhead becomes prohibitive due to latency/cost constraints.
findings.decay_rationale: Pattern is foundational; stable unless tool alternatives change.

findings.title: Docling: 97.9% Table Accuracy, Slower, Heavier
findings.summary: Docling (open-source, AI-powered layout analysis) achieves 97.9% table cell accuracy via deep learning. Trade-off: 6.28s per page (~65s per 50-page doc), 1,032MB installation, 88 dependencies. Preferred for complex PDFs, scientific papers, financial reports.
findings.invalidation_condition: If Docling's accuracy or performance changes materially.

findings.title: Unstructured: Enterprise-Grade, 88%+ Reliability, Hybrid SaaS/Self-Hosted
findings.summary: Unstructured is enterprise document processing platform with 88%+ reliability, OCR and NLP models, API-based or open-source self-hosted. Bridges SaaS and self-hosted models; offers support contracts. Slower than MarkItDown; more reliable.
findings.invalidation_condition: If Unstructured's pricing or support model changes.
findings.decay_rationale: SaaS offerings evolve rapidly; pricing and support terms change.

findings.title: Marker & MinerU: Academic/Structured Documents
findings.summary: Marker (balanced speed/structure, strong image handling) and MinerU (GPU-accelerated, heading detection, complex tables as HTML, quality approaches commercial tools) excel at academic papers and structured documents. Outperform MarkItDown on scientific/financial content; slower.
findings.invalidation_condition: If Marker/MinerU performance or availability changes.

findings.title: Version 0.1.5 (Feb 2026) Indicates Pre-1.0 API Stability
findings.summary: MarkItDown at v0.1.5. SemVer: 0.x = API instability, expect feature churn. Landscape treats as 'mature'; deep-dive correctly flags as 'PARTIALLY verified — 0.x, expect API churn.' Microsoft frames as open-source research project, not productized with SLA.
findings.invalidation_condition: If MarkItDown reaches v1.0.0 or if Microsoft offers formal SLA.
findings.decay_rationale: SemVer interpretation is stable; invalidated only by major version change.

findings.title: Community Momentum: Active Maintenance, Plugin Ecosystem
findings.summary: GitHub stats (April 2026): 117K stars, 7.6K forks, 352 issues, 286 PRs. Recent releases (v0.1.3 Aug 2024, v0.1.4 Dec 2024, v0.1.5 Feb 2026). Plugin system (v0.1.0+) enables extensions. Not abandoned; active maintenance.
findings.invalidation_condition: If maintenance velocity drops or Microsoft ends support.
findings.decay_rationale: Activity levels change quarterly; repo metrics are volatile.

contradictions_resolved.title: Path Traversal Vulnerability (CVE Details)
contradictions_resolved.landscape_statement: MarkItDown has 'path traversal vulnerability' (no CVE cited)
contradictions_resolved.deep_dive_statement: CVE-2025-11849 in mammoth dependency; directory traversal on untrusted DOCX; CVSS 9.3; fixed in mammoth 1.11.0
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Both describe SAME vulnerability. Landscape lacked CVE number; deep-dive provided full context. No contradiction—deep-dive clarified and corroborated.
contradictions_resolved.merged_finding: CVE-2025-11849 (mammoth), CVSS 9.3, MarkItDown v0.1.4+ is patched.

contradictions_resolved.title: 'Production-Ready' Claim
contradictions_resolved.landscape_statement: Treats MarkItDown as mature, production-ready tool
contradictions_resolved.deep_dive_statement: PARTIALLY verified — still 0.x, expect API churn, not production-grade with SLA
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Contradiction on terminology, not fact. Landscape is marketing-influenced; deep-dive is SemVer-correct.
contradictions_resolved.merged_finding: Stable in practice (active maintenance) but NOT formally production-ready per SemVer. Suitable for internal tools; enterprises need internal support.

contradictions_resolved.title: Office Format Support Quality
contradictions_resolved.deep_dive_statement: Office formats 'well' — PARTIALLY verified. Basic only; no structured table/list extraction; issue #41 open since 2024
contradictions_resolved.resolution: No contradiction; deep-dive provides accuracy qualification. Both correct on breadth; deep-dive adds quality detail.
contradictions_resolved.merged_finding: Supports Office formats as input (DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) but with limited structure preservation. Suitable for simple docs; inadequate for complex layouts.

must_carry_caveats.text: CVE-2025-11849 (mammoth dependency): directory traversal, CVSS 9.3, allows arbitrary file read on untrusted DOCX input. MarkItDown v0.1.4+ pins mammoth ≥1.11.0 (patched). Teams using MarkItDown 0.1.0–0.1.3 with locked old mammoth pins remain exposed.
must_carry_caveats.reason: Critical security detail; omission risks vulnerable deployments.

must_carry_caveats.text: 47.3% accuracy baseline requires fallback validation. Nearly 50% of documents may require fallback processing (Docling, Azure DI, manual review). Do NOT treat 47% as acceptable without fallback chain.
must_carry_caveats.reason: Marketing understates failure rate; fallback is operational requirement.

must_carry_caveats.text: Table extraction failure is architectural (GitHub issue #41, open since 2024). MarkItDown extracts columns separately, destroying row-column correlation. Not a bug; architectural design. Table-rich documents require Docling or post-processing.
must_carry_caveats.reason: Teams discover this late; tempering expectations prevents rework.

must_carry_caveats.text: MarkItDown MCP server (markitdown-mcp) exposes convert_to_markdown(uri) without URI validation. MCP deployments to untrusted clients require URI scheme/path allowlists upstream. Python library (convert_local, convert_response) is safe.
must_carry_caveats.reason: MCP security is deployment-specific; omitting confuses library vs. MCP risk.

must_carry_caveats.text: Version 0.1.5 (February 2026) indicates 0.x pre-1.0 API stability. Expect feature churn and breaking changes. Microsoft provides no SLA or support guarantee; enterprises should plan internal support.
must_carry_caveats.reason: Marketing suggests maturity; SemVer realities manage expectations.

must_carry_caveats.text: Frank's World blog claims 40–60% RAG accuracy improvement via heading-aware chunking. No methodology disclosed; no independent corroboration. Do NOT cite without validation.
must_carry_caveats.reason: High-confidence-sounding but unsupported claim risks misrepresenting tool capability.

gaps_critical.title: SSRF Allowlists / Rate Limits in MarkItDown URL Fetching
gaps_critical.impact: If MCP or library exposes convert(url) API, security posture assessment depends on whether built-in allowlists exist.
gaps_critical.status: UNVERIFIED — investigator searched; no documentation found.
gaps_critical.recommendation: Contact Microsoft or perform source-code audit.

gaps_significant.title: Exact Vision Model Compatibility (OCR Plugin)
gaps_significant.impact: MarkItDown-OCR lists GPT-4o, Azure OpenAI, 'OpenAI API-compatible.' Claude and open-source models untested.
gaps_significant.recommendation: Test with target models before deployment.

gaps_significant.title: Multi-Language Evaluation
gaps_significant.impact: Benchmarks focus on English. Behavior on CJK, RTL, code-heavy documents unknown.

gaps_significant.title: Performance Data on Complex Mixed Documents
gaps_significant.impact: Benchmarks exist for pure PDF, pure Office, pure HTML. Mixed-format pipelines lack systematic data.

gaps_significant.title: Total Cost of Ownership Analysis
gaps_significant.impact: No published comparison (speed gains vs. accuracy failures rework) for specific document volumes/SLAs.
gaps_significant.status: Deep-dive calculates optional costs; no comprehensive TCO model.

gaps_minor.title: Tested Throughput Limits & Cluster Deployment
gaps_minor.impact: No documented max files/sec, memory per doc, cluster guidance.

gaps_minor.title: Maintenance Velocity Metrics
gaps_minor.impact: 352 issues, 286 PRs; unclear active vs. stale. Commit frequency not quantified.
gaps_minor.recommendation: Query GitHub API for commit frequency.

invalidation_conditions: If accuracy requirements increase from ~47% to >80%, recommend Docling/Unstructured.
If corpus becomes table-heavy without fallback, recommend Docling or MinerU.
If untrusted input cannot be validated, recommend Unstructured or Docling.
If future MarkItDown release unpins or downgrades mammoth, re-assess CVE-2025-11849 exposure.
If defusedxml is downgraded or XML parsing bypassed, re-audit XXE risk.
If MCP must serve multi-tenant/untrusted clients without upstream validation, use narrow APIs or isolate.
If Azure DI or LLM API costs spike, fallback chain economics may favor Docling.
If corpus documents regularly exceed 10MB with complex layouts, empirical testing required.
If multilingual support needed, validate with CJK/RTL/code-heavy corpus.
If enterprise SLA required, MarkItDown's 0.x stability and lack of support guarantee are blockers.

staleness_summary.fastest_material_decay: F9 (MCP SSRF) — fast (6 months) — deployment-specific; can be remediated quickly.
core_findings: F1, F2, F3, F4, F7, F17 — core to recommendation
volatile_findings: F12 (Unverified 40–60% RAG claim) — volatile (90 days) — undated and unsupported.
